name: Portillo Reservoir Loop from Castril
name_original: Embalse de Portillo — circular desde Castril de la Peña
description: This difficult 13.2-mile touring cycling route circles the Embalse de Portillo from Castril de la Peña, offering stunning mountain and river
region_intro: Best touring cycling routes in Castril region traverse a landscape defined by the Sierra de Castril Natural Park, featuring river valleys, reservoir areas, and elevated terrain. The area is characterized by its natural water features, including the Castril River and Portillo Reservoir, alongside a mix of open fields and more rugged, hilly sections. These physical features provide diverse conditions for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Castril The most popular touring cycling route is Portillo Reservoir Loop from Castril, a 13.2 miles (21.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Casita del Duende Robustiano - circular gravel from Pozo Alcón, an easy 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This route features gravel sections through a natural setting. Local touring cyclists also love the Castril Village – Portillo Reservoir loop from Castril, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail leading through the Castril village area and around the Portillo Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Castril is defined by river valleys, reservoir landscapes, and the foothills of the Sierra de Castril. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy gravel paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Castril are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Castril's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Castril region?
A: The Castril region offers a diverse network of over 18 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for touring cycling routes around Castril?
A: The routes around Castril range from easy to difficult. You'll find 1 easy route, 3 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes, providing options for both casual riders and experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Castril?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy route perfect for beginners. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996275877/casita-del-duende-robustiano-circular-gravel-from-pozo-alcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casita del Duende Robustiano - circular gravel from Pozo Alcón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.2 miles (14.8 km) and features gravel sections through a natural setting.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Castril?
A: Touring cycling around Castril is defined by river valleys, reservoir landscapes, and the foothills of the Sierra de Castril. You'll encounter natural water features like the Castril River and Portillo Reservoir, alongside a mix of open fields and more rugged, hilly sections.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that feature waterfalls or significant water bodies in Castril?
A: Many routes in Castril incorporate natural water features. For a route that specifically highlights a waterfall, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Herradura – Guazalamanco Waterfall loop from Hotel Rural Los Nogales&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the region features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6598554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calavera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and several reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Clemente Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of touring routes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions to look out for along the cycling routes?
A: The Castril region is rich in natural beauty. Along your rides, you might encounter impressive gorges such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elías Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castril Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Herradura&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark. Many routes offer views of the Portillo Reservoir and the surrounding Sierra de Castril Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Castril?
A: Yes, many routes in Castril are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castril Village – Portillo Reservoir loop from Castril&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.9 miles (48.1 km) and takes about 4 hours 28 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Castril?
A: Given Castril's Mediterranean climate, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme heat than summer. Winter can be cool, and some higher elevation routes might be affected by colder conditions.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Castril?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 6 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to reservoir views, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: For advanced riders seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla Seca Shelter – Campos de Hernán Perea loop from Nava de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning 39.1 miles (63 km) with significant elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience through the region's rugged terrain.
tips: The El Portillo reservoir or El Portillo Dam is located on the Castril River, in the municipality of Castril, in the province of Granada. This reservoir belongs to the Guadalquivir Hydrographic Confederation, in the Granada area.

Castril is located on a cliff above the river that bears the same name. The town has a 13th century castle, a 16th century Renaissance church and a medieval bridge over the river. It is an ideal place for nature lovers thanks to its proximity to the Sierra de Castril Natural Park and the Portillo Reservoir.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Embalse de Portillo circular touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 21.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 7 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness'. While primarily paved, some segments may be unpaved and challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. It primarily features paved surfaces, but be prepared for some unpaved and difficult segments where you might need to dismount and push your bike. The route includes an elevation gain of around 580 meters, making it a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Embalse de Portillo circular route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the stunning Portillo Reservoir with its turquoise waters, and the dramatically perched Castril Village. The route also offers impressive landscapes of mountains and forests, and follows the Castril River through canyons and past waterfalls. Nearby, the 'Cerrada del Río Castril' gorge features a hanging bridge, wooden walkways, and a metal suspension bridge leading to a waterfall.

faqs.question: Does this touring cycling route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will encounter sections of the Acequias de la Vega de Tubos, Gran Senda Primeros Pobladores (1/6 and 2/6), Cerrada de Lézar, Puente de Lézar, Cerrada del río Castril, Camino Colada de Pozo Alcón, and Camino del Chorro.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Granada, Spain, and the challenging nature of the route, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse weather conditions in the mountains.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Portillo circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the region, it's important to consider the difficulty and terrain of this specific touring cycling route. Some sections may be challenging for dogs, especially if you need to push your bike. Always keep dogs on a leash, especially near wildlife or other users, and ensure they are fit enough for the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route or the Sierra de Castril Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Embalse de Portillo circular route itself, nor for general access to the Sierra de Castril Natural Park where it is located. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially for any specific activities or protected areas within the park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de Portillo circular route from Castril de la Peña?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Castril Village. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route. Specific parking areas may be signposted for visitors.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in Castril Village, you will find cafes, pubs, and restaurants there to refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of the ride itself, as options along the trail are limited.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for cycling the Embalse de Portillo circular route?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for the 'difficult' rating and the significant elevation gain. Some unpaved sections might require you to dismount and push your bike, so ensure your footwear is suitable for walking. Given the challenging nature, starting early in the day is recommended to avoid the midday heat and allow ample time to complete the route comfortably.
